criteria.label: Consumer Language
criteria.guidance: Reads like a real person speaking, not a marketing deck or a spec sheet.

criteria.label: Emotional Relevance
criteria.guidance: Connects to a feeling, identity or tension the consumer actually cares about.

criteria.label: Clarity
criteria.guidance: Easy to follow on first read; no jargon, no overload.

criteria.label: Coherence (Insight ↔ Benefit ↔ RTB)
criteria.guidance: Benefit answers the insight; RTB supports the benefit; one shared idea runs through all three.

criteria.label: Distinctiveness
criteria.guidance: Has a clear point of difference, not a generic 'better/improved' claim.

criteria.label: Believability of RTB
criteria.guidance: The reason-to-believe makes the benefit credible using simple, intuitive logic.

criteria.label: Strategic Fit to Search Field
criteria.guidance: Framing, angle and wording match the selected search field.

criteria.label: Single-mindedness / Focus
criteria.guidance: One core message, not three competing ones.

criteria.label: Tagline Fit
criteria.guidance: Short, memorable, and clearly tied to the concept direction.

criteria.label: Overall Screener Readiness
criteria.guidance: Holistic judgement of how this would perform in a consumer concept screener. Computed, not independently scored.
